plan.rationale: novel territory; record for later consolidation

thought.text:
The puzzle presents an intriguing challenge: "All birds can fly. Penguins are birds. Therefore penguins can fly." This conclusion seems too good (or should I say, too obvious?) to be true.

Pattern recognition kicks in as I notice that the puzzle relies on a classic example of a logical fallacy, specifically an unjustified generalization.
